question: Should the board have a separate "Backlog" column for not-yet-started features (status=draft, current_phase=inception), or just the six pipeline phase columns (Inception, Planning, Construction, Review, Testing, Delivery)?
options: Six pipeline columns only — draft features appear in the Inception column
Seven columns: Backlog + six pipeline phases — draft/unstarted features separated from active inception
Other

question: Which view should be the default when a user first opens the Dashboard (no prior selection remembered)?
options: List view (existing default — Kanban is opt-in)
Board/Kanban view (Kanban becomes the primary view, list is the alternate)
Other

question: How should the user switch between List and Kanban views?
options: In-page toggle control on the Dashboard (single route, segmented List/Board control)
Separate route (e.g. /kanban) with nav links between the two
Other

question: How long should the user's view choice persist?
options: Session only (sessionStorage — resets when browser closes)
Across sessions (localStorage — remembered permanently)
Do not persist — always start from the default view
Other

question: Should users be able to drag-and-drop cards between columns to change a feature's phase, or is the board view-only?
options: View-only — phase changes happen via the existing pipeline actions on the detail page
Drag-and-drop enabled — dropping a card in a new column triggers a phase transition
Other

question: How should features with terminal status (done, cancelled) appear on the board?
options: Show them in their current_phase column (full retrospective view)
Hide them from the board (only show active/in-flight work)
Show them but visually dimmed/greyed in their current_phase column
Other

question: When a column has more cards than fit the viewport, how should overflow be handled?
options: Vertical scroll within each column (board height bounded to viewport, column headers stay fixed)
Show a count and a "+N more" affordance that expands or navigates
Let the page scroll normally (no per-column scroll)
Other

question: What should the board show when a phase column has zero features?
options: Render the column with header + muted "No features" placeholder (pipeline shape always visible)
Hide empty columns (only show phases that have features)
Other
